DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS GARDA CL WEST, INC., AND TERENCE BRINSON'S MOTION TO COMPEL SUBPOENAED DOCUMENTS: COURT DIRECTED AMENDMENT OF THE SUBPOENA TO REQUIRE PRODUCTION OF MEDICAL RECORDS FOR A PERIOD OF FIVE YEARS PRIOR TO THE DATE OF THE SUBJECT ACCIDENT TO DATE AND SOLELY REFLECTING INJURIES TO PARTS OF THE BODY ALLEGED TO BE INJURED. PLAINTIFF SHALL BE GIVEN FIRST LOOK AT THE SUBPOENAED RECORDS AND MAY REDACT ENTRIES ASSERTED TO BE OUTSIDE THE SCOPE OF THE SUBPOENA. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO THE COURT FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW A PRIVILEGE LOG AND FORWARD SAME ALONG WITH THE REDACTED AND UNREDACTED VERSION OF THE RECORDS FOR RULINGS ON REDACTIONS. THE DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A DISCOVERY ORDER REFLECTING THE AGREEMENTS AND RULINGS REACHED AT THE HEARING. JUDGE PRO TEM: STEVE STEIN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Off Calendar Sep-10-2021 Continued To Jun-20-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-17-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. SUSAN F. REYES, Esq. of Law Offices of Susan F. Reyes (Tel: 415-982-0241) for plaintiff;
Atty. PALOMA I. MORENO-ACOSTA, Esq. of Lewis Brisbois Bisgaard & Smith LLP (Tel: 619-233-1006) for defendant Garda CL West, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GARDA CL WEST, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Garda's motion for summary adjudication of the second cause of action for negligent hiring is denied without prejudice.

On a motion for summary judgment, the moving party "bears the burden of persuasion that there is no triable issue of material fact and that he is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. . . ." (Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 850, 854.) Defendant fails to meet its burden to show that there is no triable issue of material fact. Defendant's argument that it has admitted it is vicariously liable is unsupported by any admissible evidence. Defendant solely relies on Mr. Brinson's incident report form and statement, which does not support its claim that it has admitted responsibility. (See Moreno-Acosta Decl., Exs. 3-4.) Compare Diaz v. Carcamo (2011) 51 Cal.4th 1148, 1153 ["[At trial] Defendant-employer Sugar Transport offered to admit vicarious liability if its employee Carcamo was found negligent."]; Armenta v. Churchill (1954) 42 Cal.2d 448, 456 ["[D]efendants admitted in their answer the agency and scope of employment of Dale Churchill . . . . During the trial . . . . defendant Alece Churchill again admitted her liability for all damages sustained by plaintiffs in the event that her husband was found to be liable."]; Jeld-Wen, Inc. v. Superior Court (2005) 131 Cal.App.4th 853, 859 ["All defendants moved for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim. They provided a separate statement referring to Jeld-Wen/Summit's admission in discovery and in a declaration by its manager that it, as the employer, would assume vicarious liability for any alleged negligence of its employee."].) There is no binding admission, stipulation, or statement by any of Defendant's representatives that it admits vicarious liability.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Susan F. Reyes (415-982-0241; susanfreyeslaw@gmail.com) for Plaintiff.
Paloma I. Moreno-Acosta (619-699-4990; paloma.moreno-acosta@lewisbrisbois.com) for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTs GARDA CL WEST, INC., and TERENCE BRINSON's Motion To Compel Subpoenaed Documents:

Court directed amendment of the subpoena to require production of medical records for a period of five years prior to the date of the subject accident to date and solely reflecting injuries to parts of the body alleged to be injured. Plaintiff shall be given first look at the subpoenaed records and may redact entries asserted to be outside the scope of the subpoena. Plaintiff to prepare and submit to the court for in camera review a privilege log and forward same along with the redacted and unredacted version of the records for rulings on redactions. The defendant shall prepare a discovery order reflecting the agreements and rulings reached at the hearing.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
